title: scopolia carniolica
intro: Scopolia carniolica, also known as devil's breath, is a perennial plant native to Eastern Europe and parts of Asia, belonging to the family Solanaceae. The plant contains tropane alkaloids, specifically scopolamine and hyoscyamine, which possess medicinal properties. These compounds have been used to treat various health conditions, including glaucoma, motion sickness, and gastrointestinal issues, by reducing spasms, inflammation, and improving vision. In herbal preparations, Scopolia carniolica is often combined with other ingredients to create tinctures, extracts, or ointments that can be used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
